content:
# Transient Electromagnetic

**Summary**: An active electromagnetic method that measures the decay of induced currents to determine conductivity.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] used transient electromagnetic (TEM) methods to provide high-resolution 1D models of conductivity variation for the upper 500m.

- [[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024]] used transient electromagnetic soundings to verify gravity results and characterize local structures.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024]] used transient electromagnetic soundings to verify gravity results and characterize local structures.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- The integration of geophysical data revealed lateral resistivity discontinuities and fracture zones. ([[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024, p. 20]])

- TEM soundings were used to validate the interpretation of gravity data in the area near the hot spring. ([[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024, p. 92]])
